title: EQUAL Youth Programme
text:
EQUAL Youth Programme - SupportGoWhere
EQUAL Youth Programme
Share link
Provides social support to at-risk children and youth aged between 7 and 21 years old to build Habits of Mind and develop resilience through equine-facilitated psychotherapy.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Fees payable
Referral required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

EQUAL Youth is a youth developmental programme which aims to provide social support and develop socio-emotional skills of at-risk children and youth through equine-assisted activities.

The outcomes of the programme are:
a) To develop socio-emotional skills of children and youth (e.g. impulsivity management, confidence and resilience building), and
b) To improve family functioning

Who is this for?

At-risk children and youth aged between 7 and 21 years old from mainstream schools and/or Special Education schools, and supported by other social service agencies.

What to expect?

Agency receives referrals from institutional partners including schools and/or other agencies.

Institutional partners will identify, screen and select the suitable children and youth who will benefit from EQUAL Youth Programme.

Agency will then assess the suitability of these selected children and youth based on the programme's entry criteria.

How to apply?

Write in to info@equal.org.sg

Students can be referred by schools and/or other social service agencies

Information last updated on 29 Sep 2025
